payload.text: Necesito crear una aplicación que cargando un archivo de audio, transcriba todo el contenido del audio. Estos audios son informes médicos realizados por voz, los cuales luego deben de ser trancriptos. El resultado, debe ser un documento .doc disponible para descargarse

**Conceptualizing the React App**

I'm now focusing on the React application's core functionality. I'm prioritizing the audio upload and transcription steps, leveraging the Gemini API. I need to figure out how to best structure the medical report output and enable downloadable reports. I'm exploring different libraries for the file upload, transcription handling, and report generation.


**Designing User Input and Output**

I'm now zeroing in on the user interface specifics. I want to add microphone input alongside file upload, thinking mode using the powerful `gemini-3-pro-preview` with a high `thinkingBudget`, and a focus on world-class senior front-end design using Tailwind CSS. I'm prioritizing generating a clean `.doc` output, I'm leaning toward the `docx` library but if there are limitations, I can create a robust blob-based download.


**Refining the UI Components**

I'm now deep-diving into the UI components. I'm focusing on the upload area, audio player, real-time transcription display, and structured medical report editor. I will implement action buttons for download, copy, and restart, all using Tailwind CSS. I have outlined the core steps: audio-to-base64, transcription using `gemini-3-flash-preview`, and report refinement with the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model, all using a high `thinkingBudget`. I am planning on using `docx` for `.doc` report generation.


**Confirming Report Generation Strategy**

I've decided to use a blob-based approach for generating `.doc` reports, as a full `docx` implementation seems complex without a dedicated library. I'll stick with HTML-to-Word to ensure reliability. I'm confirming that the "Thinking mode" will use `gemini-3-pro-preview` for formatting, and `gemini-3-flash-preview` is ideal for transcription. I am also planning the visuals - I am leaning towards blues, teals, and clean whites. I am building the structure: Patient Identification, Clinical History, Physical Examination, Diagnostic Impression, and Treatment Plan.

entries.description: Application metadata including name, description and required permissions for camera/microphone access.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font Awesome for iconography.
React entry point using the latest React 18+ API.
Global type definitions for the application.
Service layer for interacting with Gemini API models for transcription and medical report refinement.
Main application component managing state, layout, and processing workflow.

payload.text:
En los audios siempre se mencionan los puntos y comas, deben interpretarse y no incluirse en la transcripción. Las mas comunes son:
Punto aparte, punto y coma

**Defining Transcription Needs**

I'm currently focused on pinpointing the specific requirements for refining the medical transcription application. I've identified the need to accurately transcribe spoken punctuation, like "punto aparte" and "punto y coma," into the correct symbols. This is the main focus of my attention right now, and I'm considering the best approach.


**Refining Punctuation Handling**

I'm now focusing on a more concrete implementation plan. I've decided to update the system prompt in `services/geminiService.ts` to explicitly instruct the model on handling spoken punctuation. Specifically, I will include instructions for "punto aparte" (paragraph break) and "punto y coma" (semicolon). The goal is for the model to interpret these commands and insert the appropriate formatting, ensuring the medical terminology remains central. I anticipate this will significantly improve the accuracy of transcriptions.


**Improving Output Aesthetics**

I recognize that while this update is functional, there's an opportunity to subtly enhance the visual appeal of the output. I don't see any immediate need for visual changes, but I anticipate the "Raw Transcription" and "Structured Report" text to be more polished as a result of the changes. This will make the user experience feel significantly improved in the longer term.

entries.description: Update transcription prompt to interpret spoken punctuation commands like 'punto aparte' and 'punto y coma'.

payload.text: okey, pero el doc descargado debe ser de la transcripcion del texto unicamente formateado

**Isolating Transcription Text** I'm now focusing on extracting and formatting just the raw transcription text from the `.doc` file. I'm moving away from generating a full structured report and instead concentrating on properly interpreting punctuation cues within the text itself. The goal is a clean, formatted text output. **Refining Download Logic** I'm now prioritizing the download function, specifically. My focus is now on extracting the *uniquely formatted* transcription text. I'll swap the `downloadDoc` function to prioritize outputting the `originalText` derived from the audio transcription. While the UI can still showcase structured info, the download itself should be the clean transcription, as requested. **Adjusting Download Output** I'm now revising the download logic within `App.tsx` to prioritize the "originalText", the cleaned transcription. It's becoming the core of the downloaded document. I'll include line breaks for better readability. I'll preserve the structured UI for the "Intelligent Medical Transcription" interface. The goal is a professional-looking, single-section medical note. **Updating Download Output Again** I'm now revising the download logic further in `App.tsx` again. I'm focusing on incorporating the request for a "clean prose" transcription in the download. I'm ensuring `report.originalText` from `structuredData.originalText` will be the sole content. I'm updating `downloadDoc` to use this, which will become the core text. I'll also rename labels for clarity, so that the download is definitely the "Transcription." **Clarifying Data Flow** I'm verifying the data flow; `rawText` directly feeds `originalText` within the `refineMedicalReport` process, which is exactly as I intended. I also double-checked that the `StructuredReport` interface definition aligns with this. The `.doc` output will focus on the clean transcription. Newlines from `originalText` will be replaced with line breaks or `

` tags for improved document formatting. The goal is a clean, professional medical document output. I'm focusing on the `downloadDoc` function, so it can display the correct content. **Verifying Prompt Accuracy** I'm now revisiting the `transcribeAudio` prompt again, to confirm that it delivers a "formateado" result, meaning well-punctuated and paragraphed text, per the user's instructions. I'll modify the `StructuredReport` interface to focus on `originalText`. The goal is to retain the structured UI while prioritizing the specific download requirement. I'm focusing on these updates within `App.tsx`.

entries.description: Update downloadDoc to generate a Word document focused solely on the cleaned, formatted transcription text as requested by the user.

payload.text: se debe interpretar el audio, por ejemplo en una parte se menciona: No muestran signos de litiasis;  'no a ver ' el izquierdo presenta. Por lo que debe interpretarse y hacer las correcciones en el texto de manera adecuada de acuerdo al audio

**Improving Medical Transcription Accuracy**

I'm focusing on enhancing the AI's ability to understand medical context, especially for Spanish transcription. The goal is to move beyond simple word-for-word transcription and enable the AI to make intelligent, context-aware corrections. I'm currently working on interpreting the audio, specifically addressing nuances in medical terminology like "No muestran signos de litiasis" for accuracy.


**Refining Spanish Audio Transcription**

I'm now zeroing in on handling hesitations and self-corrections in Spanish audio. The key is in updating `transcribeAudio` to act as an expert medical editor, refining the transcription to make logical sense. This includes cleaning up phrases like "no a ver" in context, ensuring the corrected version is downloaded. I'm updating the prompt in `geminiService.ts` to explicitly handle these nuances.


**Enhancing Contextual Accuracy**

I'm now integrating a new rule for context correction into the AI's processing. It will proactively remove self-corrections and filler words to ensure medically sound and coherent transcriptions. The prompt is being updated to reflect this new capability, enabling seamless handling of phrases like "no, a ver". My aim is to deliver a refined and accurate transcription, devoid of any extraneous elements.

entries.description: Refine the transcription prompt to include expert medical editing logic, handling self-corrections (e.g., "no a ver", "perdón") and ensuring clinical coherence while following dictated punctuation commands.

payload.text:
quiero hacer estos cambios en el documento .doc:
1. los saltos de linea no dejen un salto entre lineas
2. Poner en negrita y subrayado el nombre del estudio al comienzo.
3. Finalizar con un ATTE en el medio del documento final.
4. En la linea siguiente sobre el lado derecho, Indicar: Dr. Rubén Luis Brodsky
Especialista en Radiología
M.N. 63574 - M.P. 1601

**Refining Document Formatting** I'm currently focused on the `.doc` file's appearance. First, I'm working on removing any unwanted line spacing, ensuring a clean and consistent layout. Second, the study name will be prominently displayed at the start, bolded and underlined for emphasis. I'm aiming for a professional presentation. **Revising Document Structure** I've significantly expanded the scope; the `.doc` updates require a plan now. I'm starting with the data: adding `studyName` to the `StructuredReport` type and the Gemini service to extract it. Then I'll update the `downloadDoc` function in `App.tsx`, and make a few styling changes. Finally, I will add the centered "ATTE" and signature line with doctor details to the document. I think this will be an effective, clean implementation. **Enhancing Doc Generation** I'm now streamlining the `.doc` generation with HTML styling, using `
` for single line spacing to align with the user's preference. I've noted specific styles: underlining and bolding the study name, centering "ATTE", and right-aligning doctor details. My focus is now on the `downloadDoc` function in the application, and the AI prompt for Gemini needs an update for `studyName` extraction. The new plan involves type additions, prompt updates, and formatted document handling. **Implement Doctor Details** My priority now is implementing the right-aligned, multi-line doctor details. I'm actively updating the necessary files to accommodate this new requirement within the document generation process. The code updates are in progress to ensure the details are correctly formatted and positioned.

entries.description: Updated refinement prompt to extract the study name for the report header.
Updated downloadDoc with new styling: single line spacing, bold/underlined study name, centered ATTE, and right-aligned signature for Dr. Rubén Luis Brodsky.
